reasoning: The threshold for resolution is very low: just one European country or the EU needs to announce a commitment to provide at least one HIMARS-compatible or substitute system by Dec 31, 2025. Given the extreme pressure on Europe to backfill potential U.S. aid reductions in 2025 under a new US administration, there is very strong motivation to supply long-range rocket artillery. European nations have regularly donated M270 variants and even bought US HIMARS for Ukraine in the past (e.g., Germany in 2024). While export controls from the US, Israel (for PULS), or South Korea (for Chunmoo) pose risks, the likelihood of at least one successful commitment being announced over the year is high.
